title: Wireless LAN Controller (WLC)
subtitle: Centralized Wireless Network Management Platform
description: A Wireless LAN Controller (WLC) is a network device that centrally manages multiple Wireless Access Points (APs) across an enterprise Wi-Fi infrastructure. It provides centralized configuration, security enforcement, authentication, roaming, policy control, monitoring, and high availability for wireless networks.

points.title: Centralized AP Management
points.desc: Configure and manage hundreds of Access Points from a single controller.
details: Provides centralized configuration of Access Points.
Enables bulk software and firmware updates.
Maintains consistent WLAN settings across sites.

points.title: Security & Authentication
points.desc: Integrates with WPA2, WPA3, 802.1X, RADIUS and AAA services.
details: Supports WPA2 and WPA3 wireless security.
Integrates with RADIUS and AAA servers.
Protects wireless users from unauthorized access.

points.title: Roaming & Mobility
points.desc: Provides seamless client roaming between Access Points.
details: Allows users to move between APs without disconnecting.
Improves voice and video application performance.
Enhances user mobility across enterprise networks.

points.title: High Availability
points.desc: Supports Active/Standby redundancy for uninterrupted wireless services.
